text:
Epilepsy Centre 

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 21 September 2025 

Dr Patel  
St. Cuthbert Community Health Centre  
42 Mill Lane, Newcastle upon Tyne, NE3 4HT  

Dear Dr Patel,  

Laura Bennett, DOB: 14-02-1991, Hospital No: NHS5482031 NHS No. 8374629501  
Flat 3, 19 Orchard Close, Newcastle upon Tyne, NE2 5LP  

Diagnoses: 

Symptomatic generalised epilepsy.

I reviewed Laura Bennett in clinic today with her partner present. She has a confirmed history of generalised epilepsy and remains under our service for ongoing management. She works in a care setting with irregular breaks and frequent patient transfers. Over recent months she describes cumulative fatigue, with perceived worsening of seizure control coinciding with longer shifts and disrupted sleep.

Following the recent medication adjustment, focal impaired-awareness seizures have been occurring more often, two to four times per week, whereas previously they only happened once or twice a month. She and her employer report missed cues at work and brief post-ictal confusion. She has not had any generalised tonic–clonic seizures since June but notes more frequent prodromal symptoms (sudden blankness, oral automatisms) during busy evening rounds. There have been no injuries, though she feels less confident performing manual handling tasks and has requested more consistent break scheduling.

She reports full adherence to prescribed medication and denies intercurrent illness or alcohol use. No new triggers identified beyond fatigue and irregular meals. There has been no recent requirement for emergency rescue therapy. Neurological examination in clinic today was unchanged. 

Given the above, I am concerned that her overall condition is deteriorating, with increased seizure burden impacting occupational function. We discussed workplace adjustments and the importance of regular rest periods and hydration; she will explore options with her line manager. I have arranged an expedited review, seizure diary monitoring, and liaison with our epilepsy specialist nurse for safety planning at work. Bloods for therapeutic drug monitoring and routine biochemistry were taken today; we will contact her with results.

I will see Laura Bennett again in 6–8 weeks or sooner if there is any further deterioration.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: focal impaired-awareness seizures have been occurring more often, two to four times per week
Following the recent medication adjustment, focal impaired-awareness seizures have been occurring more often, two to four times per week, whereas previously they only happened once or twice a month

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: "focal impaired-awareness seizures have been occurring more often, two to four times per week". This provides a clear current seizure frequency range. It also notes no generalised tonic–clonic seizures since June, but that does not negate the ongoing focal seizures. Per the rule to select the highest frequency when multiple types are reported, we use the focal impaired-awareness seizure frequency of 2 to 4 per week.
seizure_frequency_number: focal impaired-awareness seizures have been occurring more often, two to four times per week
reference: Following the recent medication adjustment, focal impaired-awareness seizures have been occurring more often, two to four times per week, whereas previously they only happened once or twice a month
